flavorText: Hawlucha live quietly without flocking together. They fight constantly with their natural enemies: noisy groups of Primeape.

This Pokémon uses its hard tail to make its nest by boring holes into bedrock deep underground. The nest can reach lengths of over six miles.

attacks.name: Dig
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It creates mazes in dark locations. When spotted, it flees into the ground by digging with its tail.

Since it tells time precisely, some countries consider it to be a deity of wisdom, versed in the ways of the world.

As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: Okidogi is a ruffian with a short temper. It can pulverize anything by swinging around the chain on its neck.

attacks.name: Earthen Spike
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Land Collapse
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck. If you played an Ancient Supporter card from your hand during this turn, discard 3 more cards in this way.

flavorText: Sightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. The name Great Tusk was taken from a creature listed in a certain book.

attacks.name: Mad Bite
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This special Ursaluna can see in the dark with its left eye and protects itself with mud that is as hard as iron.
It brandishes its gaping mouth in a display of fearsome strength. It raises vast quantities of sand while attacking.
It shrouds itself in sand to ward off germs. It travels easily through the sands of the desert.

attacks.name: Tornado Rush
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Tornado Rush attack does 100 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It has the peculiar power of being able to see emotions, such as joy and rage, in the form of waves.

attacks.name: Swelling Power
attacks.effect: Attach a Basic Fighting Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Magma Purge
attacks.effect: Discard up to 4 Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon is said to represent the land. It went to sleep after dueling Kyogre.
Its shell is as hard as bedrock, and it is also very strong. Its thrashing can topple a mountain.
It is born deep underground. It can't emerge until it has entirely consumed the soil around it.

attacks.name: Adjusted Horn
attacks.effect: If you don't have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It was named after a mysterious object recorded in an old book. Its body seems to be metallic.

attacks.name: Energy Feather
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Fezandipiti beats its glossy wings to scatter pheromones that captivate people and Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Munkidori keeps itself somewhere safe while it toys with its foes, using psychokinesis to induce intense dizziness.

attacks.name: Hex Hurl
attacks.effect: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: This Pokémon has characteristics similar to those of Flutter Mane, a creature mentioned in a certain book.

attacks.name: Roaring Scream
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each damage counter on this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: There has been only one reported sighting of this Pokémon. It resembles a mysterious creature depicted in an old expedition journal.

attacks.name: Magical Charm
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do 100 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Angelite
attacks.effect: Choose 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. Shuffle those Pokémon and all attached cards into your opponent's deck. If 1 of your Pokémon used Angelite during your last turn, this attack can't be used.

rules:
As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: Sylveon cuts an elegant figure as it dances lightly around, feelers fluttering, but its piercing moves aim straight for its opponents' weak spots.

attacks.name: Perfume Press
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: The scent that constantly emits from its fur is so powerful that this Pokémon's companions will eventually lose their sense of smell.
A scent pouch within this Pokémon's body allows it to create various scents. A change in its diet will alter the fragrance it produces.

attacks.name: Shadow Bind
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: It comes and goes between this world and the afterlife. People fear this Pokémon because it is said to suck in lost spirits and take them along.
Dusclops's true form is said to be a bright red eye burning within its body, but no one has ever seen such a thing.

attacks.name: Come and Get You
attacks.effect: Put up to 3 Duskull from your discard pile onto your Bench.

flavorText: Duskull's victims suffer an intense chill as the Pokémon siphons their life-force away, glaring at them with its bright red eye all the while.

attacks.name: Psych Out
attacks.effect: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Amazez
attacks.effect: Devolve each of your opponent's evolved Pokémon by shuffling the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent's deck.

rules:
As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psychic Assault
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The tip of its forked tail quivers when it is predicting its opponent's next move.

attacks.name: Volt Cyclone
attacks.effect: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Amp You Very Much
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 1 more Prize card.

attacks.effect: You may discard up to 2 Basic Energy from your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 90 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

attacks.name: Fighting Lightning
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: It concentrates the weak electric charges emitted by its cells and launches wicked lightning bolts.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Sob
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Torrential Pump
attacks.effect: You may shuffle 3 Energy attached to this Pokémon into your deck. If you do, this attack also does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Euclase
attacks.effect: Knock Out 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that has exactly 6 damage counters on it.

rules:
As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Permeating Chill
attacks.effect: At the end of your opponent's next turn, put 9 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It lowers its body heat to freeze its fur. The hairs then become like needles it can fire.

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It races around the world to purify fouled water. It dashes away with the north wind.

attacks.name: Severe Squall
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon ex. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Aquamarine
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fighting Whirlpool
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: It lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin, which is often mistaken for a mermaid's.

attacks.name: Rapid Draw
attacks.effect: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: In autumn, its body becomes more fatty in preparing to propose to a mate. It takes on beautiful colors.

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its dorsal, pectoral, and tail fins wave elegantly in water. That is why it is known as the Water Dancer.

attacks.name: Wash the Slate Clean
attacks.effect: You may put 2 Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

flavorText: It has incredible intellect and intuition. Whatever the situation, it remains calm and collected.
It is always vacantly lost in thought, but no one knows what it is thinking about. It is good at fishing with its tail.

fullDisplayName: Hearthflame Mask Ogerpon ex 017/131 Prismatic Evolutions

attacks.name: Wrathful Hearth
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dynamic Blaze
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 140 more damage, and discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Flame Tackle
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: The mane of a male Pyroar heats up to over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit during battle. Merely approaching it will cause severe burns.
The more a Litleo trains its body and spirit by battling mighty enemies, the hotter its mane will grow.

attacks.name: Burning Charge
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fighting Blaze
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Inhaled air is carried to its flame sac, heated, and exhaled as fire that reaches over 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

fullDisplayName: Teal Mask Ogerpon ex 012/131 Prismatic Evolutions

attacks.name: Myriad Leaf Shower
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.

Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Grass Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Do the Wave
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The head sticking out belongs to the fore-wyrm, while the tail belongs to the core-wyrm. The two share one apple and help each other out.
Applin lives inside an apple. When an Applin is separated from its apple, its body loses moisture and the Pokémon grows weak.
It scatters cotton all over the place as a prank. If it gets wet, it'll become too heavy to move and have no choice but to answer for its mischief.

attacks.name: Triple Spin
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It shoots cotton from its body to protect itself. If it gets caught up in hurricane-strength winds, it can get sent to the other side of the Earth.

attacks.name: Verdant Storm
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Moss Agate
attacks.effect: Heal 100 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon.

rules:
As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Leaflet Blessings
attacks.effect: Attach a Basic Grass Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, heal all damage from that Pokémon.

flavorText: The younger they are, the more they smell like fresh grass. With age, their fragrance takes on the odor of fallen leaves.

attacks.name: Itchy Pollen
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

flavorText: The pollen it releases contains poison. If this Pokémon is raised on clean water, the poison's toxicity is increased.
These Pokémon judge one another based on pincers. Thicker, more impressive pincers make for more popularity with the opposite gender.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said that on rare occasions, one of its heads will drop off and continue on as an Exeggcute.
Using telepathy only fellow Exeggcute can pick up on, they always form a cluster of six.
